title: fix: return original value in get_from_dict_or_env instead of casting to str
body_text:
Fixes #35285

The function was forcing str() conversion on return values, which broke when using enum values or other non-string types in the dict. Now returns the original value with return type changed from str to Any.

entities.name: Get From Dict Or Env
entities.description: A function that retrieves values from a dictionary or environment variables.

entities.name: Forced String Conversion
entities.description: The problematic behavior of a function converting return values to strings.

entities.name: Enum Values
entities.description: Enumeration values that were incorrectly converted to strings by the function.

entities.name: Non-String Types
entities.description: Data types other than strings that were incorrectly converted by the function.

entities.name: Original Value
entities.description: The value as it was originally, without any forced string conversion.

entities.name: Return Type Any
entities.description: The updated return type of the function, allowing it to return any data type.

relations.label: CAUSES
relations.evidence: The function was forcing str() conversion on return values

relations.label: AFFECTS
relations.evidence: which broke when using enum values

relations.label: AFFECTS
relations.evidence: or other non-string types in the dict

relations.label: RELATED_TO
relations.evidence: with return type changed from str to Any

extracted_data.summary: A fix for the `get_from_dict_or_env` function prevents forced string conversion of return values, which previously broke enum and other non-string types, by returning the original value and changing the return type to `Any`.
